2605.20525.title: NeuroQA: A Large-Scale Image-Grounded Benchmark for 3D Brain MRI Understanding
2605.20525.paper_summary: We present NeuroQA, a large-scale benchmark for visual question answering in 3D brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), with 56,953 QA pairs from 12,977 subjects across 12 datasets. It spans ages 5-104 and five clinical domains: Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, tumors, white matter disease, and neurodevelopment. Unlike prior medical Visual Question Answering (VQA) efforts that operate on 2D slices or rely on narrow diagnostic labels, NeuroQA pairs every item with a full 3D volume. It evaluates 11 clinically grounded reasoning skills across Yes/No, multiple-choice, and open-ended formats. Of the 203 templates, 131 are image-grounded (answerable from a 3-plane viewer) and 72 are image-informed (ground truth from quantitative volumetry or clinical instruments). To remove text-only shortcuts, we apply answer-distribution refinement, reducing closed-format text-only accuracy from $>$80% to 44.6%; image necessity is assessed separately through an image-grounding protocol released with the benchmark. A 38-rule deterministic pipeline and two rounds of expert review verify every QA pair against FreeSurfer measurements, metadata, or radiology report fields, with zero same-subject contradictions across templates. We conduct a clinician evaluation in which two clinicians independently assess 100 frozen test items on a three-plane viewer. On closed-format (Yes/No + multiple-choice) test-public items, the best zero-shot vision-language model and a supervised 3D CNN baseline reach 47.5% and 43.7% accuracy respectively, both below the 49.4% text-only majority-template floor. NeuroQA adopts a two-tier release with public QA pairs for open-access datasets and reproducible generation scripts for datasets restricted by data use agreements (DUAs), plus subject-level splits, a held-out private test set, and an online leaderboard.
2605.20525.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了NeuroQA，一個針對3D腦部磁共振成像（MRI）的視覺問題回答的大型基準，包含來自12,977名受試者的56,953對問答，來自12個數據集。它涵蓋了5至104歲的年齡範圍和五個臨床領域：阿茲海默症、帕金森症、腫瘤、白質疾病和神經發育。與之前基於2D切片或依賴狹窄診斷標籤的醫療視覺問題回答（VQA）工作不同，NeuroQA將每個項目與完整的3D體積配對。它評估了11種臨床基礎的推理技能，涵蓋是/否、多選和開放式格式。在203個模板中，131個是基於圖像的（可以從三平面觀察者回答）而72個是基於圖像的信息（從定量體積測量或臨床工具獲得的真實數據）。為了消除僅文本的捷徑，我們應用了答案分佈精煉，將封閉格式的僅文本準確率從$>$80%降低到44.6%；圖像的必要性通過與基準一起發布的圖像基礎協議單獨評估。一個38條規則的確定性流程和兩輪專家審查驗證每對問答與FreeSurfer測量、元數據或放射學報告字段的一致性，並且在模板中沒有同一受試者的矛盾。我們進行了一次臨床醫生評估，兩位臨床醫生獨立評估了100個冷凍測試項目，使用三平面觀察者。在封閉格式（是/否 + 多選）的測試公共項目中，最佳的零樣本視覺-語言模型和一個監督的3D CNN基準分別達到47.5%和43.7%的準確率，均低於49.4%的僅文本主要模板底線。NeuroQA採用兩級發布，為開放訪問數據集提供公共問答對，並為受數據使用協議（DUAs）限制的數據集提供可重複生成腳本，還包括受試者級別的拆分、一個保留的私有測試集和一個在線排行榜。
2605.20525.author: Mohammad H. Abbasi et.al.
2605.20525.authors: Mohammad H. Abbasi, Favour Nerrise, Shaurnav Ghosh, Ridvan Yesiloglu, Yuncong Mao, Bailey Trang, Mohammad Asadi, Merryn Daniel, Gustavo Chau Loo Kung, Ken Chang, Pavan Pinkesh Shah, Adam Turnbull, Kyan Younes, Seena Dehkharghani, Ehsan Adeli
